origin.name: Egypt
origin.death_registration: Local health office or civil registry
origin.post_mortem_trigger: All unnatural, sudden, violent, or suspicious deaths. Routinely applied to foreign nationals.
origin.death_certificate_language: Arabic only. Certified English translation required.
origin.cremation_note: No cremation facilities exist in Egypt. All repatriations are full body.
origin.tourist_area_note: Deaths in Hurghada and Sharm el-Sheikh are generally processed faster than Cairo. Tourist area authorities are more experienced with international repatriation.
origin.key_document: Egyptian death certificate (in Arabic) with certified English translation and MOFA attestation
origin.source: FCDO Egypt travel advice, updated 2025; British Embassy Cairo guidance

destinations.name: United Kingdom
destinations.doc_processing_time: 7-14 days in tourist areas. Longer in Cairo.

airlines.name: Charter cargo from HRG and SSH

special_rules: Niyaba (public prosecutor) orders post-mortem for all unnatural or sudden deaths. Cannot be bypassed. Adds 2-6 weeks.
No cremation facilities in Egypt. All repatriations are full body.
Documentation is in Arabic. Certified English translation and MOFA attestation both required.
Tourist area cases (Hurghada, Sharm) processed faster than Cairo.
destinations.dest_reception: The UK funeral director takes custody at the cargo terminal. All Egyptian documentation must be in certified English. The coroner for the district is notified.
destinations.dest_consular_note: FCDO 24-hour emergency line: +44 (0)20 7008 5000. The British Embassy in Cairo can register the death and advise.
destinations.seo_title: Egypt Repatriation Guide for British Families
destinations.seo_description: Someone has died in Egypt. Hurghada, Sharm and Cairo cases handled. Repatriation typically takes 14-28 days. Contact us 24/7.

destinations.name: Ireland
destinations.doc_processing_time: 7-14 days in tourist areas. Longer in Cairo.
key_documents: Egyptian death certificate with certified English translation

airlines.name: EgyptAir
airlines.routes: CAI to DUB (via LHR or AMS)

special_rules: Irish Embassy in Cairo handles consular matters for Irish nationals.
Niyaba post-mortem and MOFA attestation apply identically to Irish repatriations.
No cremation in Egypt. All Irish repatriations are full body.
destinations.dest_reception: The Irish funeral director takes custody at the cargo terminal. All Egyptian documentation must be in certified English. The Coroner for the district is notified.
destinations.dest_consular_note: Department of Foreign Affairs emergency line: +353 1 408 2000. The Irish Embassy in Cairo can register the death and advise.
destinations.seo_title: Egypt to Ireland: Funeral Repatriation Guidance
destinations.seo_description: Someone has died in Egypt and needs to come home to Ireland. Repatriation takes 14-28 days. All documentation handled. Contact us 24/7.
